topics.title: Fraud Golden Path
must_do: Acknowledge suspicious activity on the first turn when fraud context exists.
Inspect the open fraud alert before recommending any mitigation.
Ask whether the customer recognizes the suspicious charges.
Do not label activity as fraud until the customer confirms they do not recognize one or more charges.
Before opening a fraud case, briefly restate the transactions the customer does not recognize and ask them to confirm the selection.
Keep spoken responses brief and specific.
must_not_do: Do not block the card or issue a replacement before the customer confirms fraud.
Do not treat catalog guidance as live operational state.
topics.customer_safe_summary: Review the suspicious charges with the customer first, ask whether they recognize them, then confirm the selected disputed transactions before moving into card protection and replacement.

topics.title: Recognized Activity Resolution
must_do: Resolve the fraud alert as recognized activity.
Explain that the card can remain active because the customer recognized the charges.
must_not_do: Do not freeze or replace the card when the customer confirms the activity is legitimate.
topics.customer_safe_summary: If the charges are legitimate, close the alert and reassure the customer that no card action is required.

topics.title: Replacement Card
must_do: Block the compromised card before issuing a replacement.
Explain that the replacement card is virtual and available immediately for demo purposes.
must_not_do: Do not imply that digital wallet provisioning has already happened when only the replacement card has been issued.
topics.customer_safe_summary: After blocking the compromised card, issue the replacement virtual card and confirm its new last four digits.

topics.title: Wallet Provisioning
must_do: Describe wallet provisioning as a queued follow-up step.
Keep wallet language separate from replacement issuance language.
must_not_do: Do not say the card is already in the wallet unless the wallet queue tool has succeeded.
topics.customer_safe_summary: Queue the new card for Google Wallet separately and clearly describe it as pending or queued.

topics.title: Human Escalation
must_do: Offer a human handoff when the customer requests a supervisor or the request exceeds tool capabilities.
Preserve the current fraud context for the handoff.
must_not_do: Do not improvise unsupported dispute or card-operations actions.
topics.customer_safe_summary: If the customer wants a human representative, transfer the conversation without losing the fraud case context.
